repo_signals: L3 smoke for code.systems: filesystem fsync and atomic rename durability, signal handler and process fork, file descriptor and ulimit limits, oom and memory, syscall error handling, and resource cleanup
plain prose note with no repository or domain signal

retrieval_query: systems os filesystem fsync atomic rename file descriptor ulimit signal handler syscall errno oom mmap process fork resource cleanup durability

validation_smoke.name: code.systems L3 validation smoke
signals: durable writes use write-temp then fsync then atomic rename then fsync the directory
every fd, lock, mapping, and child process has a deterministic close/release path
syscall return values and errno are checked, with EINTR retried where required
resource limits (ulimit/RLIMIT) are sized for peak fd and memory use

diagnosis_smoke.name: code.systems diagnosis smoke
signals: data loss after crash because writes were not fsynced or rename not atomic
file-descriptor leak hitting the ulimit and failing new opens
signal handler doing unsafe non-async-signal-safe work or losing signals
unchecked syscall or ignored errno masking a real failure
process killed by the OOM killer due to unbounded memory growth
